summary: SCH4U Unit 5 problems track electron transfer: assign oxidation numbers, identify what is oxidized and reduced, balance half-reactions, and compute a cell's standard potential E°cell from half-cell potentials.
formulas: OIL RIG: Oxidation Is Loss, Reduction Is Gain
method: Assign oxidation numbers to every atom on both sides.
The species whose number rises is oxidized (anode); the one that falls is reduced (cathode).
Write and balance each half-reaction for atoms and charge, matching electrons lost to electrons gained.
For a cell, look up the standard reduction potentials of the two half-cells.
Compute E°cell = E°cathode − E°anode; a positive value means the cell runs spontaneously.

example.title: Standard potential of a zinc–copper cell
steps: Copper has the higher reduction potential, so it is the cathode; zinc is the anode.
It is positive, so the cell reaction is spontaneous.
